topic: China SG null (2009) — ISS-1829 eight-gravimeters citation hedge verification (EXP-323)
change_summary: EXP-323 integrated into wins.json/docs/index.html (commit b0b7cb93): paragraph 2 of detail_evidence hedged to attribute the eight-gravimeter/six-site campaign description to the 2013 CJG paper (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306) and contemporaneous Chinese Academy of Sciences communiqués, with transparent qualifier that we have not independently verified the precise instrument/site totals. ISS-1829 moved to closed (status:fixed). Paragraphs 1, 3, 4 byte-identical; verdict text unchanged; verdict (Refuted by Data) unchanged.
our_argument_summary: WIN-014 argues that the 2009 East Asian eclipse Chinese SG-network null result refutes the dome model rather than confirms it, because the dome's own published κ = 1.677 nT/µGal coupling applied to the documented 5–15 nT eclipse magnetic perturbation predicts a 3–9 µGal contact-phase Δg pulse — well above the 0.1–1 µGal SG post-reduction noise floor — that the network did not record. EXP-323 added a citation hedge on the campaign description (gravimeter count, site count) without altering this argument; the post-hedge prose still asserts the campaign occurred and produced a network-level null, with the refutation resting on the κ-derivation, not on exact instrument totals.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: monitor.py contains no domain handler for WIN-014. The C5 (China SG null) claim is not auto-validated by the dome's monitoring script — it appears in the WIN list as a static narrative claim. κ itself is tested via Domain #7 (live-power dashboard tile #5), but the historical 2009 null is not a comparison the script performs.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: N/A — no automation touches this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. The code_analysis.monitoring="none" tag remains accurate.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no domain or fetch logic for WIN-014. Confirmed by string search of raw-text/monitor.py for "014", "China SG", "superconducting gravimeter", and "2009 eclipse" — no matches in a comparison context. Domain #7 tests κ via live-power dashboard, not the 2009 retrospective null.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: There is no standard-physics prediction that the 2009 Chinese SG network should have recorded a contact-phase Δg pulse during eclipse totality. Standard physics expects no such pulse (eclipses do not cause measurable gravitational perturbations at SG precision). The dome model is not relabeling a standard prediction; it is making a unique prediction via κ that contradicts the null.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The Chinese SG network null result was published in CJG 2013 (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306). The dome model adopted the published null as a confirmed WIN after the fact, treating the published outcome as validation. The dome did not state a prospective prediction in advance of the 2009 measurement campaign.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The value the dome claims as confirmed is zero — adopted from the published CJG 2013 null. No derivation from dome geometry produces zero; the dome's own κ-coupling actually predicts a non-zero 3–9 µGal pulse, which is the self-contradiction this WIN now exploits as a refutation.

holes_found.description: Patch integration dropped one character: detail_evidence paragraph 2 reads "contemporaneous Chinese Academy of Sciences communiqés" (codepoints 63 6f 6d 6d 75 6e 69 71 e9 73 — missing the 'u' before 'é'). EXP-323's replacement_html had the correct "communiqués" (codepoints 63 6f 6d 6d 75 6e 69 71 75 e9 73). The typo also propagated into docs/index.html (verified by grep — 1 occurrence of communiqés, 0 of communiqués). String diff between EXP-323.replacement_html and the landed detail_evidence is exactly 1 character (length 2772 vs 2771); first-difference position is at index 776 inside the parenthetical attribution. The typo is in a French loanword inside a parenthetical aside and does not affect the argument, but it is a real defect introduced during integration and is now visible in the rendered review.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "communiqés" with "communiqués" in data/wins.json WIN-014.detail_evidence, then rebuild docs/index.html via build.js. Pattern: sed -i "s/communiqés/communiqués/g" but verify with hexdump that the replacement is byte-accurate (75 e9 not e9 alone). Single-character patch, no test impact expected. Worth a separate ISS or a one-liner self-apply at decider's discretion.

holes_found.description: After the hedge, detail_evidence describes the campaign as "approximately eight superconducting/spring gravimeters across roughly six Chinese sites" with the explicit qualifier "we have not located an independent English-language verification of the precise instrument or site totals." The tldr_evidence still reads confidently: "The eight-gravimeter network, sampling continuously at 0.1–1 µGal precision, recorded zero across all sites." Per the prompt's TLDR Review guidance, a TLDR that contradicts the evidence is major; a TLDR that is imprecise but does not mislead is minor. This is the latter — a non-science reader of the TLDR still forms a correct understanding (~8 SGs measured zero), so the inconsistency is at the imprecision end. But the hedge was deliberately applied to detail to acknowledge the verification limit; mirroring it in the TLDR would preserve internal consistency.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional, low-priority: change tldr_evidence sentence 3 from "The eight-gravimeter network, sampling continuously at 0.1–1 µGal precision, recorded zero across all sites" to "The Chinese SG network, sampling continuously at 0.1–1 µGal precision, recorded zero across all sites." Drops the unverified count from the TLDR while preserving the load-bearing facts (Chinese, SG-class precision, network-level null). If decider judges this not worth the patch cycle, defer indefinitely — does not block ISS-1829 closure.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-323 chose the conservative path: rather than fabricate or guess a Yang 2009 progress-report citation (which the c3 review hypothesized but never verified), the analyst folded the gravimeter/site count into the same 2013 CJG attribution already used for the null result, with an explicit transparency qualifier. This is the right move epistemically — citing a paper we have verified (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306) and being explicit about what we have not independently verified is more credible than inventing a citation to make a parenthetical look complete. See EXP-323.anticipated_objections[1] (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-323.json) for the reasoning trail.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The hedge is a citation fix, not a substantive concession. It does not weaken the κ-derived refutation: paragraph 3 of detail_evidence still computes Δg ≈ 3–9 µGal from documented 5–15 nT eclipse magnetic perturbations (Kim & Chang 2018, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022) through the dome's own published κ = 1.677 nT/µGal, and the load-bearing fact — that the network recorded zero across all sites at 0.1–1 µGal precision — does not depend on whether there were exactly eight or six gravimeters. EXP-323.anticipated_objections[0] ("the refutation rests on the network-level null result, not the exact instrument or site count") survives a defender pushback intact. The verdict Refuted by Data continues to hold (current_verdict_holds:true; ISS-1829 closure does not relitigate it).

deeper_analysis: Verification summary: PASS-with-typo. EXP-323 integrated cleanly into both data/wins.json and docs/index.html (commit b0b7cb93). ISS-1829 is correctly recorded as fixed in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json with fixed_via=EXP-323 and a verification_pattern that grep-matches the canonical hedge phrase. Of the four paragraphs in detail_evidence, paragraphs 1, 3, and 4 are byte-identical to pre-patch; only paragraph 2 was modified, exactly as EXP-323 promised in its summary_for_decider ("Only paragraph 2 changed (the other three paragraphs are byte-for-byte identical to the current detail_evidence)"). Adversarial check on the hedge itself: a dome defender cannot now use "you cited a campaign without proof it happened" as a rebuttal — the 2013 CJG paper is named with DOI and our acknowledged limit is the precise count, not the existence of the campaign. The κ-derivation continues to refute the null. One character was dropped during integration ("communiqués" → "communiqés"); this is a minor cosmetic defect, easily one-line patched, and the only finding in this review file requiring decider action. The TLDR consistency note is offered but explicitly downgraded to optional/low-priority per the prompt's "don't split hairs" TLDR guidance. Carry-over: ISS-1957 (major, c7 H1), ISS-1959 (moderate, c7 H3), ISS-1960 (moderate, c7 H4) remain open per monitor/decisions/open-issues.json; these are out-of-scope for this verification review and should continue through their own decider routing.

citations_verified: doi:10.6038/cjg20130306 (Chinese Journal of Geophysics 2013) — referenced in the hedge as the 2013 CJG paper; this DOI is already cited elsewhere in WIN-014 and was verified in prior cycles.
DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 (Kim & Chang 2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049) — referenced for the 39-eclipse magnetic ensemble; verified clean in c8 (ISS-1999 closure, queue_id 339).
DOI 10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101 (Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi 2013, Rep. Prog. Phys. 76, 046101) — referenced for SG precision floor; unchanged by this patch and not in scope.

summary_for_decider: Queue_id 345 (WIN-014 ISS-1829 hedge verification via EXP-323): PASS with one minor defect. Patch landed cleanly across data/wins.json and docs/index.html; ISS-1829 closure record is intact; paragraphs 1/3/4 byte-identical to pre-patch; verdict Refuted by Data unchanged. Hedge does not degrade the empirical refutation — the κ-derivation in paragraph 3 still produces the 3–9 µGal predicted pulse that the network did not record. One minor finding: "communiqués" was transcribed as "communiqés" (missing 'u') in detail_evidence and propagated to docs/index.html. EXP-323.replacement_html had the correct spelling; the character was dropped during integration. Single-character fix, no argument impact. Optional secondary note: tldr_evidence still says "eight-gravimeter network" confidently while detail now hedges to "approximately eight" — minor TLDR imprecision, not misleading per prompt's don't-split-hairs guidance; up to decider whether to mirror the hedge in the TLDR. No carry-over from c7 in this review (the open major/moderate ISSs from c7 — ISS-1957, ISS-1959, ISS-1960 — are tracked separately and not in scope for the ISS-1829 verification).
